title: Robust Rye Turkish Lamb Adana Kebap
description: A pungent and flavorful Turkish dish featuring lamb, rye, and spices, served in a scallop shell and steamed to perfection. This flexitarian-friendly meal is perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, rye flour, onion, garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Divide the lamb mixture into 4 equal portions and shape each portion into a long, thin cylinder.
Place each cylinder onto a scallop shell, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Place the scallop shells on a large piece of aluminum foil and fold the foil over the kebaps to create a packet.
Steam the kebaps over boiling water for 10 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the kebaps hot, with lemon wedges on the side.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
